proposals.statement: Under the merger agreement, Paramount will pay $31.00 per share in cash for all outstanding shares of WBD (an all-cash transaction).

source.title: Paramount press release: 'Paramount to Acquire Warner Bros. Discovery'

proposals.quote: Under the terms of the agreement, Paramount will pay $31.00 per share in cash for all outstanding shares of WBD.
proposals.locator: Transaction terms (press release dated Feb 27, 2026), opening paragraph

proposals.statement: Paramount values WBD at $81 billion in equity value and $110 billion in enterprise value at $31 per share in cash plus a ticking fee.

source.title: Paramount press release: 'Paramount to Acquire Warner Bros. Discovery'

proposals.quote: Paramount will acquire 100% of WBD for $31 per share in cash, plus the "ticking fee", valuing WBD at $81 billion in equity value and $110 billion in enterprise value.
proposals.locator: Transaction Highlights (press release dated Feb 27, 2026)

proposals.statement: If the transaction has not closed by September 30, 2026, WBD shareholders receive a $0.25-per-share 'ticking fee' for each quarter (measured daily) until closing.

source.title: Paramount press release: 'Paramount to Acquire Warner Bros. Discovery'

proposals.quote: In the event the transaction has not closed by September 30, 2026 , WBD shareholders will receive a $0.25 per share "ticking fee" for each quarter (measured daily) until closing.
proposals.locator: Transaction terms (press release dated Feb 27, 2026)

proposals.statement: WBD's proxy materials state the $31.00 per share in cash represents a 147% premium to WBD's unaffected stock price of $12.54 per share, with the unaffected price defined as the closing price on 9/10/25.

source.title: WBD DEFA14A — Shareholder Meeting Date of April 23, 2026

proposals.quote: WBD shareholders will receive $31.00 per share in cash for each share of WBD common stock they own, which represents a 147% premium to WBD’s unaffected stock price of $12.54
proposals.locator: DEFA14A (filed Mar 26, 2026), 'WBD Sets Shareholder Meeting Date' — terms paragraph; footnote i: 'Unaffected stock price defined as closing price on 9/10/25'

proposals.statement: The 147% premium is arithmetically consistent with the grounded prices: $31.00 / $12.54 = 2.47x, i.e. +147.2%, so the offer is roughly 2.5x the unaffected price. (Note: a figure of '139%' sometimes cited for this deal is not what the company discloses; the disclosed and arithmetically correct premium is 147%.)

On February 26, 2026 — the last trading day before the public announcement of the Paramount merger — WBD's closing price was $28.80 per share, already far above the $12.54 unaffected level (reflecting the prior Netflix deal and the contested auction).

source.title: WBD DEFM14A (Paramount merger proxy)

proposals.quote: On February 26, 2026, the last trading day prior to the public announcement of the Merger, the closing price for the shares of WBD Common Stock on Nasdaq was $28.80 per share.
proposals.locator: Market Price and Dividend Data of WBD Common Stock (p. 138)

proposals.statement: WBD entered the Paramount (PSKY) merger agreement after its Board determined PSKY's proposal to acquire WBD for the Merger Consideration represented a 'Company Superior Proposal' as defined in the amended-and-restated Netflix merger agreement; the Netflix merger agreement was then terminated and the Netflix merger abandoned.

source.title: WBD DEFM14A (Paramount merger proxy)

proposals.quote: We entered into the Merger Agreement with PSKY following a determination by the WBD Board that the proposal submitted by PSKY to acquire WBD for the Merger Consideration represented a “Company Superior Proposal,” as defined in the A&R Netflix Merger Agreement.
proposals.locator: Background of the Merger / introductory summary

proposals.statement: In connection with terminating the amended-and-restated Netflix merger agreement, PSKY paid Netflix a $2.8 billion cash termination fee (the 'Netflix Termination Fee') on behalf of WBD, and the special meeting for the Netflix merger was cancelled.

source.title: WBD DEFM14A (Paramount merger proxy)

proposals.quote: In connection with the termination of the A&R Netflix Merger Agreement, PSKY, on behalf of WBD, paid Netflix a termination fee of $2.8 billion in cash (the “ Netflix Termination Fee ”), and we canceled the special meeting of WBD stockholders for the Netflix Merger.
proposals.locator: Background of the Merger / introductory summary

proposals.statement: The WBD Board's stated reasons for the merger lead with the all-cash consideration and premium: the consideration is an all-cash amount of at least $31.00 per share providing certainty of value and liquidity immediately upon closing.

source.title: WBD DEFM14A (Paramount merger proxy)

proposals.quote: The Merger Consideration is an all-cash amount of at least $31.00 per share of WBD Common Stock, providing WBD’s stockholders with certainty of value and liquidity immediately upon the Closing.
proposals.locator: Reasons for the Merger — 'Consideration Offered and Premium to Unaffected Trading Price'

proposals.statement: The WBD Board cited cash certainty as a reason: all-cash consideration removes valuation risk associated with stock consideration — a key contrast with the part-stock Netflix proposal.

source.title: WBD DEFM14A (Paramount merger proxy)

proposals.quote: The certainty of all-cash consideration removes any valuation risk for WBD stockholders associated with consideration that consists in whole or in part of stock.
proposals.locator: Reasons for the Merger — 'Consideration Offered and Premium to Unaffected Trading Price'

proposals.statement: The WBD Board also noted a downside of the all-cash structure: because the merger is all-cash, WBD stockholders will no longer participate in WBD's or the combined company's future earnings or growth after closing.

source.title: WBD DEFM14A (Paramount merger proxy)

proposals.quote: Given that the Merger is an all-cash transaction, following the consummation of the Merger, WBD’s stockholders will no longer participate in WBD’s or the combined company’s future earnings or growth.
proposals.locator: Reasons for the Merger — potential negative factors

proposals.statement: The fairness opinion supporting the Paramount merger was a joint opinion of Allen & Company and J.P. Morgan dated February 27, 2026; WBD did NOT request and Evercore did NOT render a fairness opinion as to the Merger Consideration.

source.title: WBD DEFM14A (Paramount merger proxy)

proposals.quote: WBD did not request that Evercore Group L.L.C.
proposals.locator: Opinion of Allen & Company LLC / Opinion of J.P. Morgan (header note re Evercore)

proposals.statement: In Allen & Company / J.P. Morgan's joint discounted cash flow (sum-of-the-parts) analysis, Allen & Company used discount rates of 8.0% to 9.5% for the Streaming & Studios Business and 6.75% to 8.0% for the Discovery Global Business.

source.title: WBD DEFM14A (Paramount merger proxy)

proposals.quote: The present values (as of December 31, 2025) of the cash flows and terminal values were then calculated by Allen & Company using selected ranges of discount rates of 8.0% to 9.5% in the case of the Streaming & Studios Business and 6.75% to 8.0% in the case of the Discovery Global Business.
proposals.locator: Opinion of Allen & Company LLC — Discounted Cash Flow Analysis

proposals.statement: In the same sum-of-the-parts DCF, J.P. Morgan used discount rates of 8.5% to 9.5% for the Streaming & Studios Business and 7.0% to 8.0% for the Discovery Global Business.

source.title: WBD DEFM14A (Paramount merger proxy)

proposals.quote: Morgan using selected ranges of discount rates of 8.5% to 9.5% in the case of the Streaming & Studios Business and 7.0% to 8.0% in the case of the Discovery Global Business.
proposals.locator: Opinion of J.P. Morgan — Discounted Cash Flow Analysis

proposals.statement: Allen & Company's discounted cash flow analysis indicated an implied equity value per share reference range for WBD that brackets the $31.00 Merger Consideration at its high end.

source.title: WBD DEFM14A (Paramount merger proxy)

proposals.quote: This analysis indicated the following approximate implied equity value per share reference range for WBD, as compared to the Merger Consideration: Implied Equity Value Per Share Reference Range Merger Consideration $21.16 – $31.91 $31.00
proposals.locator: Opinion of Allen & Company LLC — Discounted Cash Flow Analysis (implied range $21.16–$31.91 vs $31.00)

proposals.statement: The $31.00 all-cash price sits at or above the high end of the financial advisors' DCF reference range (Allen & Company DCF $21.16–$31.91, with $31.00 only marginally below the $31.91 top), implying WBD shareholders are being paid essentially full intrinsic value with little standalone-DCF upside left on the table — consistent with a contested-auction outcome rather than a price anchored to a discount-to-value entry.

Paramount discloses NO specific dollar or run-rate synergy figure in its deal announcement; it instead presents valuation 'on a fully synergized basis' — stating the deal values WBD at 7.5x 2026 EBITDA on a fully synergized basis.

source.title: Paramount press release: 'Paramount to Acquire Warner Bros. Discovery'

proposals.quote: On a fully synergized basis, this values WBD at 7.5x 2026 EBITDA.
proposals.locator: Transaction Highlights (press release dated Feb 27, 2026)

proposals.statement: Paramount expects net debt-to-EBITDA of 4.3x at closing 'on a synergized basis,' with a path to investment grade within three years — again expressing leverage on a synergized rather than reported basis without quantifying the synergies.

source.title: Paramount press release: 'Paramount to Acquire Warner Bros. Discovery'

proposals.quote: At closing, we expect to have a net debt-to-EBITDA of 4.3x on a synergized basis, with a clear path to investment grade credit metrics within three years of closing.
proposals.locator: Transaction Highlights (press release dated Feb 27, 2026)

proposals.statement: Paramount's stated synergy basis is qualitative: it cites technology/infrastructure streamlining to 'generate financial and operating efficiencies and eliminate redundancies,' but attaches no dollar amount, timing, or cost-vs-revenue split to these efficiencies.

source.title: Paramount press release: 'Paramount to Acquire Warner Bros. Discovery'

proposals.quote: By streamlining the technological underpinning of every aspect of the combined company's businesses, we will improve the user experience, generate financial and operating efficiencies and eliminate redundancies.
proposals.locator: Strategic and Financial Benefits — Technology / Infrastructure

proposals.statement: Because Paramount expresses both valuation (7.5x 2026 EBITDA) and pro-forma leverage (4.3x net debt/EBITDA) only 'on a fully synergized basis' yet discloses no synergy dollar amount, timing, or cost-vs-revenue mix, the deal's headline multiple and leverage cannot be independently checked, and the synergies-vs-premium test cannot be quantified from disclosure — a key finding: the synergy case is asserted but not sized. (By contrast, the competing Netflix bid did quantify synergies — see deal_24.)

Paramount's stated strategic rationale, in CEO David Ellison's words, is to honor the two companies' legacy while 'accelerating our vision of building a next-generation media and entertainment company.'

source.title: Paramount press release: 'Paramount to Acquire Warner Bros. Discovery'

proposals.quote: Discovery has been guided by a clear purpose: to honor the legacy of two iconic companies while accelerating our vision of building a next-generation media and entertainment company.
proposals.locator: David Ellison quote (press release dated Feb 27, 2026)

proposals.statement: Paramount frames the strategic driver as combining complementary streaming platforms and world-class studios to create value for audiences, partners and shareholders.

source.title: Paramount press release: 'Paramount to Acquire Warner Bros. Discovery'

proposals.quote: By bringing together these world-class studios, our complementary streaming platforms, and the extraordinary talent behind them, we will create even greater value for audiences, partners and shareholders
proposals.locator: David Zaslav quote (press release dated Feb 27, 2026)

proposals.statement: Paramount sizes a key strategic asset of the combination as a film library of more than 15,000 titles plus thousands of hours of television programming, and commits to 15 theatrical feature films per year per studio.

source.title: Paramount press release: 'Paramount to Acquire Warner Bros. Discovery'

proposals.quote: The combined company will own a film library of more than 15,000 titles and thousands of hours of television programming.
proposals.locator: Strategic and Financial Benefits — Compelling Portfolio of IP and Content

proposals.statement: The competing (later-abandoned) Netflix deal was a cash-and-stock transaction valued at $27.75 per WBD share, with total enterprise value of approximately $82.7 billion (equity value $72.0 billion) — and acquired only WBD's Streaming & Studios business, following the separation of Discovery Global.

source.title: Netflix newsroom — 'Netflix to Acquire Warner Bros.' (Total EV $82.7B / Equity $72.0B)

proposals.quote: The cash and stock transaction is valued at $27.75 per WBD share (subject to a collar as detailed below), with a total enterprise value of approximately $82.7 billion (equity value of $72.0 billion).
proposals.locator: Netflix newsroom release — 'Transaction Details and Timing'

proposals.statement: Under the Netflix terms, each WBD shareholder would receive $23.25 in cash plus $4.50 in Netflix common stock per WBD share — i.e. a part-stock structure, unlike Paramount's all-cash $31.00.

source.title: Netflix newsroom — 'Netflix to Acquire Warner Bros.'

proposals.quote: Under the terms of the agreement, each WBD shareholder will receive $23.25 in cash and $4.50 1 in shares of Netflix common stock for each share of WBD common stock outstanding at the closing of the transaction.
proposals.locator: Netflix newsroom release — 'Transaction Details and Timing'

proposals.statement: Netflix DISCLOSED quantified synergies for its WBD bid (a direct contrast to Paramount's un-sized synergy case): Netflix said it expected at least $2-3 billion of cost savings per year by year three and expected the transaction to be accretive to GAAP EPS by year two.

source.title: Netflix newsroom — 'Netflix to Acquire Warner Bros.'

proposals.quote: The Company also expects to realize at least $2-3 billion of cost savings per year by the third year and expects the transaction to be accretive to GAAP earnings per share by year two.
proposals.locator: Netflix newsroom release — synergy/accretion paragraph

proposals.statement: The WBD board's flip from Netflix's $27.75 cash-and-stock (Streaming & Studios only) to Paramount's $31.00 all-cash whole-company bid delivered both higher headline value and cash certainty: $31.00 is ~$3.25/share (+11.7%) above the $27.75 Netflix headline, is entirely cash (eliminating the stock-collar/valuation risk the board flagged), and covers 100% of WBD rather than leaving holders with a separate Discovery Global stub — the board's own stated reasons emphasize exactly these certainty-of-value and all-cash points.
